title: Cyclomatic Complexity of Functions Should Not Be Too High
description: Excessive cyclomatic complexity of functions can make it hard to understand and maintain the code.
message: This function has a high cyclomatic complexity.

noncompliant.code: def foo(x): if x > 0: return x * 2 else x - 1

compliant.code: def bar(x): if x > 0: return 2 * x

why_is_this_an_issue: Excessive cyclomatic complexity can make it hard to understand and maintain the code, increasing the risk of bugs and errors.
how_to_fix: Refactor the function to reduce its cyclomatic complexity.

logic.function_code:
def cyclomatic_complexity_check(node):
    import ast
    import McCabe
    tree = ast.parse(node['value'])
    complexity = McCabe.mccabe(tree)
    return complexity > 10

metadata.title: Cyclomatic Complexity of Functions Should Not Be Too High
